title: question about evas
wikitext:
[]: question about evas             Quote Post by Unit007 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 4:45 pm If evas are supposedly giant organic beings why do they need battery power or need to be plugged in like unit 01 and 00?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:00 pm Because, although they are organic beings, they lack a heart (or if they do have a heart, lacks the ability) to pump blood (and as we all know, blood carries the nutrients that give beings energy) through their body. Now, how they use the energy to power the Evas I do not know, but what I speculate is that the cord, instead of keeping a continuous flow of energy, give large shocks of energy that pumps the blood.

[]:             Quote Post by Travosaga » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:02 pm It's never really explained in the show. The angels contain S2 engines which give them perpetual power. Even though the Evas are clones of this, they lack the engines and don't seem to have the ability to generate them on their own like the Angels do. I suspect that they may have been manufactured that way on purpose so that there would be a failsafe if the units ever went out of control. Of course, that doesn't seem to stop unit 01 from going berserk and running without power for short periods. The Evas aren't strictly biological creatures. They are bio-mechanical. tl;dr - It's unknown at this point.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:23 pm Welcome Unit007. For future reference, topics like this should go into the "Discussion" sub-forum or "Chit-chat" if you think it doesn't warrant it. Unit007 wrote: If evas are supposedly giant organic beings why do they need battery power or need to be plugged in like unit 01 and 00?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Apr 07, 2011 6:08 pm Don't forget, there are numerous inorganic systems that are embedded into the Evangelion that certainly require power. I highly doubt those systems are, or could be powered by the Evangelions' metabolism. Especially considering that, with the exception of that one episode, Evangelions never eat.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 07, 2011 6:24 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Don't forget, there are numerous inorganic systems that are embedded into the Evangelion that certainly require power. I highly doubt those systems are, or could be powered by the Evangelions' metabolism.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Apr 07, 2011 7:26 pm toe mash wrote: "Nutrients"? You mean oxygen ? I hope for your sake biology just wasn't a mandatory subject or something

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Apr 07, 2011 8:43 pm toe mash wrote: ..."Nutrients"? You mean oxygen ? I hope for your sake biology just wasn't a mandatory subject or something

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Fri Apr 08, 2011 4:52 am symbv wrote: toe mash, I think it is you whom I am worried about your biology lesson... Blood does carry nutrient. Stryker was talking about Blood, not Red Blood Cells.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Apr 08, 2011 4:58 am toe mash wrote: Oh come on, symbv. When people say "blood" they mean the blood cells, unless stated otherwise, no need to be pedantic about it. And he mentioned the heart so obviously the issue is oxygen, not "nutrients", considering it's inside the heart that the blood cells absorb oxygen (or the hemoglobin inside them if you have to be picky). It's like saying a car doesn't have an engine, so obviously the problem is the wiring.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Apr 08, 2011 5:13 am toe mash wrote: ..."Nutrients"? You mean oxygen ? I hope for your sake biology just wasn't a mandatory subject or something

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Fri Apr 08, 2011 5:26 am Ornette wrote: If you assume that Evas are aerobic.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 08, 2011 8:14 am toe mash wrote: The point is he mentioned heart, so the matter at hand was the oxygen. I'm *pretty* sure that if someone didn't have a heart, not receiving oxygen would be a tad more of an issue than a lack of vitamins and minerals.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Apr 08, 2011 2:23 pm Reichu wrote: Oxygen is only more of an issue because a lack of it is more immediately fatal, but blood is also absolutely critical for delivering sustenance to the cells and carrying wastes away. Can we move on?

[]:             Quote Post by Unit007 » Fri Apr 08, 2011 2:38 pm First off sorry for posting in the wrong forum. I realize that evas are not purely biological beings but even still when they run out of power they go completely dead. No life whatsoever right? Unless they go berserk. You would think that they would still be able to have some sort of function instead of just sitting there as they get wrecked. Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Fri Apr 08, 2011 2:44 pm It IS explained enough in the show IMO. The S2 engine is supposed to be the fruit of life. One of the major ideas in the show (and Bible itself) is that anyone who possesses fruit from the tree of life (S2 engine) and fruit from the tree of knowledge (uh, knowledge) becomes (like) a god. The angels all have S2 engines (fruits of life), which give them unlimited energy (in the Bible it's everlasting life), and the Lilin (Lilith too? i don't remember) possess knowledge. Since the Eva's are essentially humans, made by humans, presumably they don't naturally possess a true S2 engine. Therefore, they need some sort of external power. Plus, as reichu mentioned, there are lots of non-organic components to the Evas. That's also why it's such a big deal when Unit 01 uh, "gets" it's own S2 engine.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Fri Apr 08, 2011 3:20 pm Unit007 wrote: First off sorry for posting in the wrong forum. I realize that evas are not purely biological beings but even still when they run out of power they go completely dead. No life whatsoever right? Unless they go berserk. You would think that they would still be able to have some sort of function instead of just sitting there as they get wrecked. Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 08, 2011 5:06 pm Unit007 wrote: I realize that evas are not purely biological beings but even still when they run out of power they go completely dead. No life whatsoever right?

[]:             Quote Post by Unit007 » Fri Apr 08, 2011 5:15 pm Reichu wrote: They're based on bioengineered super-humans (Seeds of Life), so when the Evas run out of power it's more like they go into a form of bio-suspension. The only way to technically kill an Eva is to critically damage the core, since the Eva's "life force" is based around the soul, and the core is the soul's bodily container.
summary:
[]: question about evas             Quote Post by Unit007 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 4:45 pm If evas are supposedly giant organic beings why do they need battery power or need to be plugged in like unit 01 and 00?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:00 pm Because, although they are organic beings, they lack a heart (or if they do have a heart, lacks the ability)
